title: Abilitiy to sum subitem by category
excerpt:
Description

posts.author: Pan (hongpan zhou)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Ability to summary subitem by it’s category. E,g, we log order items, each order contains our summer product and winter product - which each sku qty data goes into subitem. I’d like to have the ablitliy to sum how many summer product and how many winter product is the total for each order.

What are you trying to achieve

Sum subitem by category

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Pan
You can achieve this using Make Automation

Create a scenario that runs whenever subitems are created or updated. Make will:

  • Fetch all subitems under the parent order

  • Read the Category (e.g., Summer / Winter) and quantity for each subitem

  • Calculate totals per category

  • Write those totals back to the parent item (for example into number columns like “Summer Total” and “Winter Total”)

This gives you a live summary per order, even though native features don’t support summing subitems by category.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
hongpan zhou:

Description

Ability to summary subitem by it’s category. E,g, we log order items, each order contains our summer product and winter product - which each sku qty data goes into subitem. I’d like to have the ablitliy to sum how many summer product and how many winter product is the total for each order.

What are you trying to achieve

Sum subitem by category

Hi @Pan

If you’re on a Pro or Enterprise plan, you could try using the Workflow feature, the AI smart conditions might help you summarize subitems by category.

Another option worth exploring is a marketplace app like Workflow Blocks: Formula (it’s free), which can handle more flexible calculations like summing quantities based on categories such as summer vs winter products.
